articles.title: AirPods, Touch Bars, and the rest of Tim Cook’s legacy
articles.description: We knew at some point Tim Cook would step down from his position as Apple's CEO. Over the last year, it has become increasingly obvious that John Ternus was his likely successor. The news this week was still a surprise, though - and this year's succession could lead to some important changes at the most […]

articles.title: The Trump phone still isn’t real
articles.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. We're back to being ignored, and the phone seems no closer to an actual launch. Last week Trump Mobile overhauled its website, in the process officially revealing the updated design […]

articles.title: I don’t think Gwyneth Paltrow knows what a peptide is
articles.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent every Friday from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. Opt in for Optimizer here. These days, it seems I cannot escape peptides. Online, I've been assaulted by videos of shirtless Chads injecting […]

articles.title: A new Republican privacy bill could be ‘worse than no standard at all’
articles.description: Congress is once again attempting to pass a national data privacy law. But while it would introduce new protections in some states, it would weaken privacy rights in others - and it's missing several elements that privacy advocates deem necessary. The SECURE Data Act is the product of a Republican data privacy working group led […]

articles.title: Musk vs. Altman is here, and it’s going to get messy
articles.description: Elon Musk cofounded OpenAI, and then flounced off in a huff when he wasn't anointed CEO, leaving Sam Altman as the last power-hungry man standing. Now, Musk is back with a lawsuit, and a trial is scheduled to start in Oakland, California, on April 27th. Theoretically, it's a legal case about whether OpenAI defrauded Musk. […]

articles.title: Instagram has launched another Snapchat clone
articles.description: Instagram is testing a new dedicated app that's focused around Snapchat-like photo sharing features. The app, called "Instants," was launched in Italy and Spain yesterday, Business Insider reports, and allows users to send each other disappearing photos that are available for 24 hours and can be viewed only once during that window. The app is […]

articles.title: China’s DeepSeek previews new AI model a year after jolting US rivals
articles.description: Chinese AI company DeepSeek released a preview of its hotly anticipated next-generation AI model V4 on Friday, saying that the open-source model can compete with leading closed-source systems from US rivals including Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI. DeepSeek says V4 marks a major improvement over prior models, especially in coding, a capability that has become central […]

articles.title: Prestigious photo contest answers ‘what is a photo?’
articles.description: We love to muse over how "real" photography is defined here at The Verge now that generative AI is so prolific, and the World Press Photo competition might have the answer. The prestigious award celebrates the best of photojournalism, where capturing reality is paramount. The winning entry for 2026 - "Separated by ICE," captured by […]

articles.title: Saros is pure action nirvana
articles.description: The alien world of Saros feels like it has been touched by King Midas. The sky is golden after another impossibly frequent solar eclipse; rocks, specifically those of the precious resource Lucenite, radiate a shimmering amber. Even the body of our gruff hero Arjun Devraj (played by Rahul Kohli) is liable to turn deep, opulent […]

articles.title: US arrests soldier who allegedly made $400K on Maduro Polymarket bets
articles.description: We knew someone made over $400,000 on suspicious Polymarket bets around the US operation to capture Venezuelan president Nicolas Maduro, but now we have a name: Gannon Ken Van Dyke. The US Attorney for the Southern District of New York announced Thursday that Van Dyke is in custody, on several charges, including using confidential government […]

tech.summary:
🔬 今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI竞争白热化**：中国DeepSeek在震撼美国竞争对手一年后，预览其新一代AI模型；马斯克与奥特曼之间的AI领域对抗正式拉开序幕，预计将变得混乱激烈。

2. **苹果库克时代遗产盘点**：多家媒体回顾蒂姆·库克任期内的标志性产品（如AirPods、Touch Bar）及苹果整体发展轨迹，引发对其领导遗产的广泛讨论。

3. **美国政治与科技政策动态**：特朗普手机传闻被证实不实；共和党新隐私法案被批评“比没有标准更糟”；特朗普政府试图削弱《濒危物种法》的努力遭遇阻碍。

4. **社交媒体与产品更新**：Instagram再次推出类似Snapchat的功能；美国士兵因在Polymarket上押注马杜罗相关赌注获利40万美元而被捕；保时捷因电动化前景减弱出售布加迪股份。

5. **太空与航天进展**：阿尔忒弥斯III火箭准备就绪；月球门户空间站主要模块出现腐蚀问题，令人尴尬；SpaceX正加速向AI公司转型。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：**DeepSeek发布新一代AI模型**，标志着中国AI在一年后持续对美国科技巨头构成挑战，全球AI竞争格局进一步加剧。

ai.summary:
🔬 今日AI动态摘要

1. **DeepSeek-V4正式发布，百万Token超长上下文成亮点**：DeepSeek-V4发布预览版，支持百万Token超长上下文，由PPIO首批上线并提供开箱即用服务，华为云率先适配，标志着国产大模型在长上下文能力上取得突破。

2. **人形机器人生态加速发展**：优必选发布“Thinker cosmos”平台，加码开发者生态，推动人形机器人从研发走向规模化应用，同时JiuwenClaw发布“Team Skills”技能新范式，强调协调工程在机器人协作中的关键作用。

3. **自动驾驶商业化持续推进**：Mobileye 2026财年一季度营收同比增长27%，显示自动驾驶技术正加速落地；同时，一个AI“通用底座”被100%主流车企选择，表明汽车行业正形成统一的AI基础设施标准。

4. **AI应用向情感与生产力双线拓展**：有团队开发出“AI小猫”产品，强调情绪价值与生产力结合；荣耀发布WIN游戏本等PC新品，AI能力全面融入消费电子设备，推动PC生态智能化。

5. **开发者工具与边缘AI部署日趋成熟**：Transformers.js被用于Chrome扩展开发，展示浏览器端运行AI模型的可行性，进一步降低AI应用门槛。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：DeepSeek-V4的发布不仅以百万Token上下文能力打破闭源模型垄断，更通过华为云等国产芯片适配，强化了自主AI生态的协同效应。

articles.description: China will strengthen innovation by developing high-tech industries with military technologies to boost military-civil integration, a move that aims to cultivate new growth drivers and boost the economy, said a recent State Council guideline.

articles.title: Dangerous stunts get thumbs-down from video platforms
articles.description: Multiple social media and video streaming platforms will limit who can view videos that depict dangerous stunts - and may even prohibit such material - in an effort to discourage imitators.

articles.description: The Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank announced on Monday a $250 million loan for a natural gas project in Beijing, the bank's first such investment in China, to help cut coal use and improve air quality in the capital area.

articles.description: China will hold an annual memorial for the victims of the Nanjing Massacre, in the eastern city of Nanjing Wednesday.

articles.title: Beijing’s Haidian district halts removal of building signs
articles.description: Urban management authorities in Beijing’s Haidian district have suspended the removal of signs and billboards from buildings, and have not made a decision when work would resume.

articles.title: PLA Navy stations women on Nansha Islands for the 1st time
articles.description: The People’s Liberation Army has sent 10 of its female members to join the garrison guarding the Nansha Islands in the South China Sea, according to the PLA Navy.

articles.title: Forum tackles urban problems for sustainable future
articles.description: A forum tackling the more challenging problems in urban development gathered people in this area on Saturday to raise awareness of greener and more sustainable urban development.

articles.title: Emojis frown on corruption
articles.description: An emojis package highlighting the eight-point austerity rules has become a hit on the internet.

articles.title: Suzhou police catch gang selling fake Herbalife products
articles.description: Police in eastern China said on Monday they have smashed an operation that made more than 20 million yuan ($3 million) from selling fake Herbalife products.

articles.title: Jack Ma Foundation pledges millions to boost rural education
articles.description: The Jack Ma Foundation has pledged to spend at least 300 million yuan ($45 million) in the next decade to staff rural schools with qualified teachers.

articles.title: New Party delegation from Taiwan visits mainland
articles.description: The director of the State Council's Taiwan Affairs Office met with a delegation from Taiwan's New Party on Monday to promote peaceful development of cross-Straits relations.

articles.title: NGO helps epileptic boy realize racing dream in Zhejiang
articles.description: An NGO helped an epileptic boy from eastern China realize his dream to ride a race car on an international circuit on Monday.

articles.title: Six killed in Hebei bath house fire
articles.description: Six people were killed on Sunday afternoon in a fire at a bath house in Yongnian district of Handan, Hebei province, authorities said on Monday.

articles.title: Residents mobilized to protect environment in Tibet
articles.description: Residents of southwest China's Tibet autonomous region have been mobilized for government-paid patrolling jobs to protect the ecosystem on the "roof of the world."

articles.title: Israeli feels special connection to Harbin
articles.description: A young foreign man walks around the city, giving big hugs to the strangers he meets on the streets in Harbin, capital of Northeast China’s Heilongjiang province.

articles.title: Honor mobile phone leading in AI speed
articles.description: Many commentators have deemed 2017 to be year one for the artificial intelligence smartphone as iPhone X and Honor V10 launched into the highly competitive but uniform cellphone market. So will an intelligent phone be the next great breakthrough?

articles.title: Xi’an security guard dies trying to save suicidal woman
articles.description: Police in Xi’an, Shaanxi province, are investigating the death of a security guard who tried to catch a woman who jumped from the 11th floor of a building, Huashang Daily reported on Monday.

articles.title: 'Naughty' elephant damages two vehicles in SW China
articles.description: A wild Asian elephant turned a highway in southwest China's Yunnan province into its playground, damaging a minibus and a sedan Saturday afternoon.

articles.title: Algerian boy, 12, wins Shenzhen talent contest
articles.description: A 12-year-old Algerian boy was crowned winner of the eighth Shenzhen Expats Chinese Talent Competition on Sunday.

articles.title: Duke Kunshan starts search for bachelor’s degree students
articles.description: Duke Kunshan University, a joint venture between Duke University in the US and China’s Wuhan University, has started to enroll its first class of undergraduate students.

articles.title: Former Chongqing Party chief under investigation
articles.description: Sun Zhengcai, the former Party chief of Chongqing, is being investigated on allegations of bribery, State prosecutors said on Monday.

articles.title: Top procuratorate investigates Sun Zhengcai
articles.description: The Supreme People's Procuratorate (SPP) of China announced on Monday that it has started investigation into Sun Zhengcai, former secretary of Chongqing Municipal Committee of the Communist Party of China (CPC).

articles.title: Xi stresses nonstop efforts to fight undesirable work styles
articles.description: Xi Jinping, general secretary of the Communist Party of China Central Committee, stressed that the fight against undesirable work styles should not be stopped.

articles.title: World's largest automated container terminal starts operation in Shanghai
articles.description: As part of the Shanghai Free Trade Zone, the system can operate completely unmanned.

articles.title: China launches communication satellite for Algeria
articles.description: China launched Algeria's first communication satellite, Alcomsat-1, into a preset orbit from the Xichang Satellite Launch Center in the southwestern province of Sichuan early Monday.

articles.title: Chinese, Algerian presidents hail successful launch of communications satellite
articles.description: President Xi Jinping and his Algerian counterpart Abdelaziz Bouteflika exchanged congratulatory messages on the successful launch of Alcomsat-1 in the Xichang Satellite Launch Center in China's southwestern province of Sichuan early Monday.

articles.title: Autopsy: Jiang suffered several injuries before the fatal stab
articles.description: The trial of the alleged murderer of Chinese student Jiang Ge began at 10 am on Monday in Tokyo. It will last seven days and the verdict is expected on Dec 20.

articles.title: Asia's tallest wooden pagoda destroyed
articles.description: The 16-story structure was said to be the tallest wooden pagoda in Asia. No casualties were reported in the incident. The cause of the blaze is under investigation.

articles.title: Guizhou: Western province looks to the future of big data
articles.description: While located in the remote southwestern part of China, Guizhou leads the development of Big Data technology in recent years.

cctv.summary:
🔬 今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. **民生与公共服务聚焦**：教育、医疗等收费问题成为社会关注重点，同时南京大屠杀幸存者最年长者去世，引发对历史记忆与传承的思考。
2. **绿色发展与生态治理**：国际组织赞扬中国绿色复兴成果；引水工程推动环境改善并创造就业；亚投行贷款支持北京煤改气项目，体现生态与经济协同发展。
3. **军事与科技双轨推进**：中俄举行潜在导弹战联合演练；更多军事技术转向民用领域以提振经济，显示国防与民用技术融合趋势。
4. **反腐败与作风建设**：重庆前高官接受腐败调查；习近平强调彻底根除不良工作作风，持续释放全面从严治党信号。
5. **外交与区域关系**：习近平外交思想推动中国与世界更紧密联系；台湾新党承诺改善两岸关系；中国为阿拉伯国家发射首颗卫星，拓展国际科技合作。

今日最值得关注的发展是：**中俄导弹战演练与军事技术民用化同步推进，折射出中国在安全与发展之间寻求动态平衡的战略取向。**
